name: Echoing Cave Chamber
Description: A large open chamber exists within the caverns, echoing sound amongst the towering stalagmites and hanging stalactites. Here on occasion, a winged figure is spotted, letting out a piercing scream that deafens travellers.

theScene: Proceeding through the winding, twisted pathways of the caverns, you happen across the massive, yawning entrance of a completely darkened chamber.

As you reach what seems to be the halfway point of the path, the darkness becomes so complete that you can't even see the trail anymore, but before you start to panic, you notice the dim glow of an exit on the far end of the cavern, and let out a sigh of relief.

But as soon as you do, the sounds of an audibly wet {i}crunch{/i} echoes through the cavern from somewhere off to your left, making your heart leap to your throat as the chamber slowly falls silent once more...
Head off the path to find the sound's origin...

You freeze, trying to stay as still as possible as you strain your ears to try and listen for whatever it was that made that noise, feeling your heart hammer in your chest as the tension builds while the sole sound of dripping water fills the air. Or... At least you {i}think{/i} it's water...

You jump as another wet sounding crunch echoes throughout the chamber off to your left once more, making your skin crawl at the visceral sounds, but try as you might to strain your eyes in the darkness, you can't even begin to try and figure out what's out there.
You let out a small breath you didn't know you were holding as your heart races, and you realize that you're not making any progress by just standing here in the pitch blackness of the cavern...

Taking a deep breath to calm your nerves, you slowly walk towards the exit as the white path under you slowly starts to reappear once more, taking extra care to make as little noise as humanly possible as all of you focus is spent on listening behind you.

You almost make it to the exit without any issues until a high pitched earsplitting shriek shatters the silence of the room, making your skin crawl with a primal surge of terror as you instinctively start to sprint towards the well lit tunnel before you.

Nothing stops you as you surge out of the blackened chamber, and only once you're far enough into the illuminated, relative safety of the tunnel do you stop and take a moment to catch your breath, feeling cold sweat drip down your brow as the blood curdling cry still faintly echoes throughout the caverns.

You notice two branching paths ahead of you as you recover, but your heart leaps once more as a new, droning wail radiates from one of the trails ahead of you, seemingly calling back in response to the first one, and you immediately straighten and take the opposite path, hoping that you won't run into whatever made either of those noises...
